info.title: KlickTipp API
info.description:
This OpenAPI specification describes the KlickTipp REST API.

It is intended for developers and integrators who want to interact directly with KlickTipp to manage contacts, tags, custom fields, opt-in processes, and related automation workflows. The API can be used standalone or through third-party platforms.

The specification focuses on correct request formats, authentication mechanisms, response structures, and error handling to ensure reliable and predictable integrations.

## Base URL

The base URL for the KlickTipp API production environment is:

```
https://api.klicktipp.com/
```

All endpoints defined in this specification are relative to this base URL.

## Getting Started

To access the API, you must authenticate and maintain a valid session.

1. **Authentication**  
   Most endpoints require cookie-based authentication. Use the login endpoint with your KlickTipp username and password to obtain a session cookie. This cookie must be sent with all subsequent requests.

2. **Request Preparation**  
   Replace all placeholder values such as email addresses, IDs, or timestamps with valid test data. Invalid formats are a common source of errors.

3. **Response Handling**  
   The API returns structured error codes and payloads. Always validate HTTP status codes and response bodies programmatically.

## Authentication Overview

The KlickTipp API supports multiple authentication models depending on the use case.

- **Username and password** authentication via session cookie is typically used for internal tools and administrative workflows.
Documentation: [KlickTipp REST API](https://www.klicktipp.com/support/knowledge-base/api-function-overview/)

- **Developer key and customer key** authentication is intended for long-running, production-grade, or multi-tenant integrations and does not rely on sessions.  
Documentation: [Developer + Customer Key Integration](https://www.klicktipp.com/support/knowledge-base/api-connection-with-developer-and-customer-key/)

## Response Format

The KlickTipp API uses JSON as its response format.

All endpoints defined in this specification are expected to be consumed as JSON and return JSON responses, including all meta information such as timestamps and structured error details.

## Data Formats and Validation

Date and time values must always be provided as Unix timestamps in seconds. Formatted date strings and locale-specific formats are not supported.

Incorrect field formats are a frequent cause of validation errors. Always ensure that values match the expected field type definitions.

## Error Handling

Errors are typically returned with HTTP status code 406 and include a numeric error code in the response body. Integrations should handle these errors explicitly and provide meaningful diagnostics.

This specification is designed to help you build stable, predictable, and maintainable integrations with the KlickTipp platform.

post.description: Logs into the API and retrieves session details for further interactions.

requestBody.description: User credentials (username and password) for authentication.

200.description:
Success Response.

Returns session details and account information.

Response contains a session cookie in the `Set-Cookie` header.

Response also contains `session_name` and `sessid` in the body for manual usage.

get.description:
Lists the IDs and names of all data fields.

**Notice:** data field IDs in this endpoint are prefixed with `field`.
Gets the ID and the name of a data field.

Lists the IDs and names of all opt-in processes.

**Notice:** The Id without a name is the Default Double Opt-In present in each KlickTipp Account.
Returns the complete data of an opt-in process.

post.description: Search the redirection URL for a specific opt-in process.

get.description: Lists the IDs of all contacts.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by subscriber status (pending, subscribed, unsubscribed)

parameters.name: bounceStatus
parameters.description: Filter by bounce status (hardbounce, softbounce, spambounce, nobounce)

post.description: Adds a new contact. If a contact with the same email already exists, it will be updated.

get.description: Returns the complete data of a contact.

post.description: Unsubscribes a contact, preventing further communication.
Adds one or more tags to a contact.
Removes a tag from a contact.
Returns the contact ID for an email address.
Returns the IDs and tagging timestamps of all tagged contacts.

get.description: Lists the IDs and names of all tags.
Returns the name and description of a tag.

delete.description: Deletes a tag and removes it from all contacts.

email.description:
Valid email address and must contain `@`.

Either `email` or `smsnumber` is required.
Email address must be unique per contact.

smsnumber.description:
Phone number in international format.

Either `email` or `smsnumber` is required.
SMS number must be unique per contact.

  Accepted input:
  - `+4912345678`
  - `004912345678`

  Stored format:
- `004912345678`

listid.description:
Opt-in process ID.

If provided, this opt-in process is used.
If omitted, the predefined double opt-in process is triggered automatically, which may trigger an email to the contact.

Retrieve available opt-in process IDs via `GET /list`.

tagid.description:
Tag ID assigned to the contact.

Used for segmentation and automation triggers.
Retrieve available tag IDs via `GET /tag`.

fields.description: Additional custom fields for the subscriber. Keys must match the field names configured in KlickTipp. Each field expects a specific data format depending on its field type. - `Line`: Single-line text without line breaks. Example: `Alex Example` - `Paragraph`: Text with paragraphs. Use `\n` to separate paragraphs. Example: `Dies ist ein Text.\nMit Absatz.` - `Number`: Integer value. No dot, no comma. Example: `42` - `Decimal Number`: Decimal value. Output always uses a dot as decimal separator. Example: Input `1999` → Output `19.99` - `URL`: Valid web address. Must start with `http://` or `https://`. Example: `https://klicktipp.com` - `Time`: Unix timestamp between `0` and `86399` (seconds since midnight). Example: `52200` (14:30 GMT) - `Date`: Unix timestamp in seconds. Date formats like `DD.MM.YYYY` are not supported. Example: `1715126400` (08.05.2024 GMT) - `Date & Time`: Unix timestamp in seconds. Do not send formatted date strings or values with spaces. Example: `1715603400` (13.05.2024 12:30:00 GMT) - `HTML`: HTML-formatted content. Only use this type if the field explicitly allows HTML. Example: `Hello World`

newemail.description:
Valid email address and must contain `@`.

Either `email` or `smsnumber` is required.
Email address must be unique per contact.

newsmsnumber.description:
Phone number in international format.

Either `email` or `smsnumber` is required.
SMS number must be unique per contact.

  Accepted input:
  - `+4912345678`
  - `004912345678`

  Stored format:
- `004912345678`

fields.description: Additional custom fields for the subscriber. Keys must match the field names configured in KlickTipp. Each field expects a specific data format depending on its field type. - `Line`: Single-line text without line breaks. Example: `Alex Example` - `Paragraph`: Text with paragraphs. Use `\n` to separate paragraphs. Example: `Dies ist ein Text.\nMit Absatz.` - `Number`: Integer value. No dot, no comma. Example: `42` - `Decimal Number`: Decimal value. Output always uses a dot as decimal separator. Example: Input `1999` → Output `19.99` - `URL`: Valid web address. Must start with `http://` or `https://`. Example: `https://klicktipp.com` - `Time`: Unix timestamp between `0` and `86399` (seconds since midnight). Example: `52200` (14:30 GMT) - `Date`: Unix timestamp in seconds. Date formats like `DD.MM.YYYY` are not supported. Example: `1715126400` (08.05.2024 GMT) - `Date & Time`: Unix timestamp in seconds. Do not send formatted date strings or values with spaces. Example: `1715603400` (13.05.2024 12:30:00 GMT) - `HTML`: HTML-formatted content. Only use this type if the field explicitly allows HTML. Example: `Hello World`

status.description: Filter by subscriber status (pending, subscribed, unsubscribed)

bounceStatus.description: Filter by bounce status (hardbounce, softbounce, spambounce, nobounce)

BadRequestResponse.description:
Bad Request Response (400).

Check your request for malformed or missing parameters.

example: The key "username" must be a string.

UnauthorizedResponse.description:
Unauthorized Response (401).

Check username and password, or possibly other required parameters.

AccessDeniedResponse.description:
Access Denied Response (403).

Your account does not have the required permissions to access this resource.

NotFoundResponse.description:
Not Found Response (404).

The specified resource could not be found.

NotAcceptableResponse.description:
Not Acceptable Response (406).

Check the response content for further error details.

In some cases, a detail error code may be returned:

- `4` - Subscription not found
- `5` - Email address validation failed
- `7` - Subscriber email not found
- `8` - Field validation failed
- `9` - Subscriber not subscribed
- `10` - Subscriber update failed
- `30` - Email address change not allowed
- `31` - Untag smarttag not allowed
- `32` - Email address SMS number required
- `100` - Invalid API key
- `401` - User not found
- `402` - List not found
- `403` - Tag not found
- `404` - User group not found
- `405` - Campaign not found
- `406` - Tier not found
- `501` - Email address required
- `502` - Username required
- `503` - Password required
- `504` - First name required
- `505` - Last name required
- `506` - Username exists
- `507` - Email address exists
- `508` - User update failed
